content.full_text:
April 28, 1978

Mrs. Raymond Ashmore
Los Angeles County Federation
of Republican Women
619 South New Hampshire
Los Angeles, California 90005

Dear Mrs. Ashmore:

It has come to our attention that certain information contained in
the June-July 1976 California Federation of Republican Women Bulletin
is erroneous. Since the Bulletin has been furnished to your affili-
ated clubs, we are writing to notify you of the inaccuracies so that
corrective steps may be taken to avoid, and attend if necessary, in-
correct filings by those clubs.

First, with regard to determining which payments are considered
campaign contributions, the Bulletin states "Donations to a Republican
Party headquarters are not considered campaign contributions." This
statement is in direct contradiction with the Fair Political Practices
Commission's ("Commission") regulation defining contribution, 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18215 (b) (1). (A copy of that regulation is en-
closed, for your information.) Under that regulation, contribution
is:

     any monetary or non-monetary payment for which
     full and adequate consideration is not made to
     the donor that is received by ...[a]n official
     committee of a political party, including a state
     central committee, county central committee,
     assembly district committee or any subcommittee
     of such committee.

The conclusion that all payments to a party's county central com-
mittee are reportable as contribution was also reached in Commission
opinion 75-188, 2 FPPC Ops. 130-132, requested by Francis Willmarth.
Therefore, if any of your affiliated clubs relied on the advice in
your bulletin and did not report payments to the central committee as
contributions, amended campaign statements should be filed.

Second, the Bulletin indicates that campaign statements (Form 420)
do not have to be filed unless contributions of $500 or more are made
to candidates or committees. Once a committee files a Statement
of Organization and qualifies as a committee under Government Code
Section 82013 (a), campaign statements must be filed if any contri-
butions are received and/or expenditures made. The only way a com-
mittee under Government Code Section 82013 (a) can relieve itself
from an obligation to file campaign statements is by filing a State-
ment of Termination.

Los Angeles County Federation
of Republican Women
April 28, 1978
Page Two .


On March 3, 1977, the Commission adopted a regulation, 2 Cal. Adm.
Code Section 18404, on termination of committees, a copy of which
is also enclosed. Under that regulation, a committee under Government Code Section 82013 (a) may terminate only when the treasurer
files a statement of termination, signed under penalty of perjury,
stating that the committee:

     1.  has ceased to receive contributions or make
         expenditures and does not anticipate receiving
         contributions or making expenditures in the
         future;

     2.  has eliminated or has declared that it has no
         intention or ability to discharge all of its
         debts, loans received and other obligations;

     3.  has no surplus funds; and

     4.  has filed all required campaign statements
         disclosing all reportable transactions.

As a result of this regulation which became effective on April 30,
1977, a committee that received contributions of $500 and filed a
Statement of Organization prior to April 30, 1977, and was still
active as a committee (by virtue of receiving contributions or
making expenditures) on April 30, 1977, should be advised that
campaign statements are required to be filed for all reporting
periods, regardless of the amount of contributions received or
expenditures made, until the committee files a Statement of Termination. Campaign statements are not required to be filed for nonelection (semi-annual) reporting period unless the committee has
received reportable contributions or made reportable expenditures.

It has also come to our attention that some of your clubs may have
filed prematurely a Statement of Organization before they qualified
as a committee under Government Code Section 82013 (a) (by receiving
reportable contributions of $500 or more in a calendar year). If
clubs that filed a Statement of Organization have never received
contributions of $500 or more in a calendar year earmarked for
political purposes, it is the opinion of the staff that these committees should be permitted to withdraw their Statements of Organization by filing a letter with the Secretary of State's Office
stating that the club has never qualified as a committee pursuant
to Government Code Section 82013 (a) and that the Statement of
Organization was incorrectly filed.

Los Angeles County Federation
of Republican Woman
April 28, 1978
Page Three

with regard to the proposed 1977 Campaign Law and The Federation fact
sheet which you sent me on April 20, 1978, I have several specific
comments:

Under the heading, State I.D. Numbers and the Fair Political Practices
Commission (FPPC), the following items are incorrect:

Item 1. Clubs require an I.D. number if they receive
$500 or more in reportable contributions in a calendar
year.

Item 2. As I stated in the second paragraph of this
letter, a payment to a District Central Committee is
reportable as a contribution. (However, if you are
referring to a payment to the District Central Com-
mittee by a local club for the local club's rental
of office space from the Central Committee, then
full and adequate consideration is provided and the
payment is not reportable as a contribution. From
the language in item 2, it was unclear which situ-
ation you were describing.)

Item 3. I.D. numbers are obtained by filing a
Statement of Organization (Form 410), within 10
days of receipt of $500 or more in contributions,
with the Secretary of State's Office, Political
Reform Division, P.O. Box 1467, Sacramento, Cali-
fornia 95807. Copies of the form are available
from the Secretary of State's Office and from the
offices of city clerks and county clerks/registrars
of voters. The I.D. number must be placed on all
campaign statements filed by a club which is a
committee under Government Code Section 82013 (a).

Under Good News for Club Treasurers, item 2, for clubs required to
file campaign statements, revenues such as those listed in item 1
would be shown as Miscellaneous Receipts on line 3 of the campaign
statement's summary page. A description should be attached describ-
ing the materials. Campaign statements are not filed with the
Commission.

I hope this information is helpful to you. If you have any questions
on these or other matters, please contact me. Incidentally, I am
enclosing a set of the Commission's revised fact sheets some of which
you could copy and provide to your affiliated clubs.

Sincerely,

Alan Herndon
Compliance Representative
Technical Assistance and
Enclosures
cc: Dick Bryant

LOS ANGELES COUNTY

FEDERATION OF REPUBLICAN WOMEN
Affiliated with
NATIONAL FEDERATION OF REPUBLICAN WOMEN

619 South New Hampshire Avenue                    Los Angeles, California 90005               (213) 381-6981


                                                 April 20, 1978
President
Mrs. Raymond Ashmore
6250 Darrington Place
Arcata, California 95521
(213) 884-0571

First Vice President
Mrs. T. A. Keehan                               Mr. Al Hearnddon
15263 Newcastle                                 Fair Political Practices Commission
Encino, California 91316                        1100 "K" Street
(213) 342-0792                                  Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Second Vice President
Mrs. Vincent Bordelon                           Dear Mr. Hearnddon;
22318 Alguras Road
Woodland Hills, Calif 91364                     As a result of our phone conversation this day, enclosed
(213) 887-7111                                  please find a copy of our letter to Mr. Bryant and a copy
                                                of the material to be edited.  (The original issue of the
Third Vice President                            material was printed on green paper in 1977 and re-printed
Mrs. Harold Haro                                on gold paper in 1978).
1755 Campus Road
Los Angeles, Calif 90031                        If the information is incorrect in any way, it is most
(213) 256-1729                                  important that we have the edited changes as soon as
                                                possible so that we may correctly inform the clubs.
Fourth Vice President
Mrs. Miles C. Pine
5009 Seaside Heights Dr.                                                Sincerely,
Rancho Palos Verdes, Ca 90274
(213) 377-5528

Recording Secretary
Mrs. Walter Daudy
21233 Placentia Cyn Rd                          Mrs. Charles Rudd
Newhall, Calif 91321                            Secretary to the Executive Committee
(805) 259-3883

Membership Secretary
Mrs. John Riobo                                 CC: Mrs. Edward Rossman
11944 Mccoy Drive                                   Campaign Reporting Chairman, LACFRW
La Mirada, Calif 90638
(213) 943-5551

Treasurer
Mrs. Home Gould
[illegible]
Los Angeles, Calif
(213) [illegible]

1977

LOS ANGELES COUNTY FEDERATION OF REPUBLICAN WOMEN

CAMPAIGN REPORTING - WORKSHOP
by
William McCormick, Controller, RCCLAC

CAMPAIGN LAW AND THE FEDERATION

Federation Classification

As of 1974, the Federation is classified as a political organization, not a
non-profit organization.

I.D. Numbers and Banks

1. Clubs do NOT need I.D. numbers for commercial bank accounts, because the
Federation is a political organization.
2. Only clubs who open savings accounts (a practice not recommended) need an
I.D. number, because the interest accrued on the account must be reported.
3. If the bank still insists upon an I.D. number for a commercial account,
refer to IRS Revenue Ruling #74-21 effective November 1, 1973. That
is the final and official ruling.

State I.D. Numbers and the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC)

1. Clubs do not need a California I.D. number UNLESS they contribute more
than $500 per calendar year to state and/or local candidates, or to any
central committee (Assembly District, County and/or State).
2. If a club financially supports an Assembly District Central Committee
headquarters, the money is not considered part of any $500 contribution,
because the club is receiving value for its money, (rent etc.).
3. F.P.P.C. I.D. numbers are obtainable from Secretary of State, Sacramento,
95814 or the Registrar Recorder, 808 N. Spring St., L.A. (Form #610).
4. A clubs normal bank account may be used from which to deposit or withdraw
funds in support of state and local candidates, Assembly District, and/
or A.D., County or State Central Committees.

Federal I.D. Numbers and the Federal Election Commission (F.E.C.)

1. Clubs do NOT need to report to the F.E.C. UNLESS they contribute $1000
or more per calendar year to federal candidates.
2. Clubs who have decided not to support federal candidates financially and
wish to be removed from F.E.C.'s mailing list of report forms, can do so
by filing a termination report dated from the last time a club filed a
report. A termination report space is shown on every report for this
purpose. A club will them be removed from F.E.C.'s mailing list.
3. F.E.C. I.D. numbers are obtainable from F.E.C., Washington, D.C. 20463
(Form #1).
4. Clubs do NOT need to maintain a separate bank account for federal contributions UNLESS they plan to support federal candidates in the total
of $1000 or more per calendar year.

Good News for Club Treasurers

1. For the purpose of arriving at a $50 political contribution, treasurers
no longer need to post revenues from dues, rummage sales, patriotic

-more-

Campaign Reporting con't.

jewelry, book sales, lunches, seminars, fashion shows, or other Ways and
Means projects.
2. For clubs who must report to F.P.P.C. for other reasons, such as revenues as listed in item 1 above, would be shown as Miscellaneous Revenues
and need not be itemized.
3. Treasurers may now accept individual member's checks for L.A.C.F.R.W.
functions and replace them with one check payable to L.A.C.F.R.W.

Good News for Club Presidents

1. Presidents are no longer officially responsible for the requirements of
campaign law.
2. Presidents being presidents however, are morally responsible to be hyperaware of reporting requirements, to pass on information to the treasurer
and to help her treasurer in any way possible.

Miscellaneous Fund-Raising Suggestions

1. It is imperative that clubs having fund raisers such as rummage sales,
Las Vegas Nights, etc. where money is collected from the public, apply
for special sales tax permits from the Board of Equalization. If clubs
have any question as to whether a permit is needed for any fund-raising
event, a call to the Board of Equalization, 5110 Sunset Blvd., L.A.
90027, Phone: 663-8181, and a request for a copy of Regulation #1595
should provide the answer.
2. The term "drawing" or "opportunity" should be used rather than "raffle"
for prizes of chance. Gambling is still subject to local control, but a
"drawing" is not considered gambling as such.

#

LOS ANGELES COUNTY

FEDERATION OF REPUBLICAN WOMEN
Affiliated with
NATIONAL FEDERATION OF REPUBLICAN WOMEN

619 South New Hampshire Avenue        Los Angeles, California 90005        (213) 381-6981


April 6, 1978
President
Mrs. Raymond Ashmore
9250 Darrington Place
Arieta, California 91331
(213) 894-0230

First Vice President
Mrs. T. A. Keeshan
25035 Navaone
Encino, California 91316
(213) 342-9702

Second Vice President
Mrs. Vincent Bordelon
22318 Algonass Road
Woodland Hills, Calif 91364
(213) 887-7461

Third Vice President
Mrs. Harold Hare
755 Campos Road
Los Angeles, Calif. 90041
(213) 256-0520

Fourth Vice President
Mrs. Miles C. Pine
5603 Seaside Heights Dr
Rancho Palos Verdes, Ca 90274
(213) 377-0528

Recording Secretary
Mrs. Walter Dowdy
21233 Placenta Cyn. Rd.
Newhall, Calif. 91321
(805) 259-2883

Membership Secretary
Mrs. John Rielo
11441 Maves Drive
La Mirada, Calif. 90638
(213) 941-7851

Treasurer
Mrs. Heinz Gruber
7335 West Ninetieth
Los Angeles, Calif. 90045
(213) 645-2771


Richard Bryant
Campaign Reporting Division
Department of Registrar-Recorder
County of Los Angeles
808 North Spring Street
Los Angeles, Ca. 90012

Dear Mr. Bryant:

Enclosed is the information distributed to our Los Angeles County
Federated Clubs. As noted, it was prepared in January 1977 and as
no corrections or changes were indicated to us by Mr. McCormick,
was again distributed in January 1978 to the Unit Clubs.

Would you please correct whatever mis-information is included in
the item and return to us as soon as possible so that no other
Federated Club will be in the precarious position that Rancho
La Habra finds itself.

Your earliest attention and response will be most gratefully
appreciated.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Mrs. Charles Rudd
Secretary to the Executive Committee

CC: Mrs. Edward Rossman,
Campaign Reporting Chairman, LACFRW

Enc.

sections.question_synthetic: The Los Angeles County Federation of Republican Women requested clarification on several California Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) regulations regarding campaign contribution reporting, committee filing requirements, and compliance obligations for affiliated Republican clubs.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC clarified that donations to party committees are reportable as contributions contrary to information in the Federation's bulletin; campaign statements must be filed once a committee receives contributions regardless of amount; committees can only terminate by meeting specific statutory requirements; and several other corrections to the Federation's fact sheets regarding I.D. numbers, contribution thresholds, and reporting procedures were provided.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is primarily an FPPC response letter (April 28, 1978) with attached incoming request materials from LACFRW (April 20, 1978) and their fact sheet. No formal question/conclusion sections exist; instead FPPC provides systematic corrections to multiple items. Confidence slightly reduced due to incomplete document text indicated by '[... truncated]' at end.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The Los Angeles County Federation of Republican Women requested clarification on several California Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) regulations regarding campaign contribution reporting, committee filing requirements, and compliance obligations for affiliated Republican clubs.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC clarified that donations to party committees are reportable as contributions contrary to information in the Federation's bulletin; campaign statements must be filed once a committee receives contributions regardless of amount; committees can only terminate by meeting specific statutory requirements; and several other corrections to the Federation's fact sheets regarding I.D. numbers, contribution thresholds, and reporting procedures were provided.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC compliance letter from Alan Herndon correcting erroneous information provided by the Los Angeles County Federation of Republican Women to affiliated clubs regarding campaign contribution reporting, committee formation, and filing requirements under California law.
